---
title: "Suits Viewership Report"
output:
  html_document:
    theme: default
    toc: true
    toc_float: true
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = FALSE, message = FALSE, warning = FALSE)
```

# *Suits* — Viewership Report

## 1. Description

*Suits* is an American legal drama television series created by Aaron Korsh. The show premiered on June 23, 2011, on USA Network. It follows Mike Ross, a brilliant college dropout, who begins working as a law associate for Harvey Specter despite never attending law school. The series focuses on corporate law cases and relationships within the firm.

---

## 2. Show Image

![Suits Title Card](https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/en/7/7f/Suits_title_card.png)

---

## 3. Basic Statistics

- Total seasons: 9  
- Total episodes: 134  
- Premiere viewership: ~4.64 million  
- Final season viewership: ~1.1 million  
- Overall trend: gradual decline in audience over time  

---

## 4. Load Libraries

```{r libraries}
library(ggplot2)
```

---

## 5. Viewership Data

```{r data}
seasons <- 1:9
viewers <- c(4.2, 3.5, 3.0, 2.5, 2.2, 1.8, 1.5, 1.3, 1.1)

df <- data.frame(seasons, viewers)
```

---

## 6. Viewership Over Time

```{r plot-viewers}
ggplot(df, aes(x = seasons, y = viewers)) +
  geom_line() +
  geom_point() +
  labs(
    title = "Average Viewership of Suits by Season",
    x = "Season",
    y = "Viewers (millions)"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 14, face = "bold"))
```

---

## 7. Season-to-Season Changes

```{r changes-data}
changes <- diff(viewers)

df_changes <- data.frame(
  seasons = seasons[-1],
  changes = changes
)
```

```{r plot-changes}
ggplot(df_changes, aes(x = seasons, y = changes)) +
  geom_line(color = "steelblue") +
  geom_point(color = "steelblue", size = 3) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "gray50") +
  labs(
    title = "Change in Viewership Between Seasons",
    x = "Season",
    y = "Change in Viewers (millions)"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 14, face = "bold"))
```

---

## 8. Observed Trends

The viewership of *Suits* shows a steady decline over time.

```{r calculate-declines}
# Calculate specific declines for reference
decline_1_to_3 <- viewers[1] - viewers[3]
decline_3_to_5 <- viewers[3] - viewers[5]
decline_6_to_9 <- viewers[6] - viewers[9]
total_decline <- viewers[1] - viewers[9]
```

- From Season 1 (`r viewers[1]` million) to Season 3 (`r viewers[3]` million), viewership decreased by **`r decline_1_to_3` million**.
- Between Season 3 and Season 5, it dropped further by **`r decline_3_to_5` million**.
- From Season 6 (`r viewers[6]` million) to Season 9 (`r viewers[9]` million), the decline was **`r decline_6_to_9` million**.

Overall, the show lost approximately **`r total_decline` million viewers** from its early seasons to its conclusion. Despite this decline, it maintained a consistent audience throughout its run.

---
